title: NamedStmt.QueryRow swallows binding errors differently than database/sql conventions
body: NamedStmt.QueryRow in named.go returns &Row{err: err} when bindAnyArgs fails, since sqlx can't produce a *sql.Row with a preset error. This is documented in a comment, but callers who do row := stmt.QueryRow(arg); err := row.Scan(&dest) sometimes miss that the bind error only surfaces on Scan, not immediately -- worth calling out more prominently in the top-level package doc.

title: reflectx Mapper cache keyed only by reflect.Type can collide for identical anonymous structs
body: StructMap caching in reflectx/reflect.go uses reflect.Type as the cache key; two separately declared anonymous structs with identical field layout share the same reflect.Type identity in Go, which is fine, but structs with the same name defined in different packages under vendoring/replace directives have historically caused confusing 'missing destination name' errors when the wrong cached StructMap is reused after a type gets reflect.Type-deduped by the runtime.

title: compileNamedQuery does not reject a named parameter that collides with a positional bindvar style
body: named.go's compileNamedQuery scans for :name style placeholders and rewrites them per-driver, but if a query mixes :name syntax with a literal PostgreSQL ::type cast (e.g. `price::numeric`), the regex can misinterpret `::type` as an empty named parameter, producing a broken rebound query for sqlx.Rebind(sqlx.NAMED, ...).

title: sqlx.In does not document behavior for an empty slice argument
body: sqlx_context.go / sqlx.go wire up sqlx.In() and Rebind() for expanding slice bindvars, but passing an empty slice (len==0) for an IN clause produces a query like 'WHERE id IN ()' which is invalid SQL in most drivers, and there's no guard or documented error for this common footgun.

title: types.NullTime does not implement driver.Valuer for zero time.Time consistently
body: types/types.go's NullTime wraps sql.NullTime-like behavior, but scanning a zero-value time.Time{} through Value() sends a non-NULL zero-time to the DB instead of NULL, which differs from how database/sql's own sql.NullTime treats a Valid=false struct. Worth aligning behavior or documenting the difference in types/doc.go.
